title: Design System: Vaccine Stock Alert System

headline.purpose: Page titles only, one per screen (e.g. "Facility Dashboard — Stock Levels").

title.purpose: Card titles, modal titles, vaccine names within a status card.

body.purpose: Form labels, table cells, button text, paragraph copy — the workhorse size.

label.purpose: Uppercase table headers, status-pill text, section captions.

shadows.name: hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Reserved for a card that is itself a click target. No card in the current app uses it — every card today is a static display surface.

shadows.name: modal-overlay
shadows.purpose: Separates the Modal from the dimmed backdrop behind it — one of two floating, untethered elements allowed a resting shadow.

shadows.name: toast-shadow
shadows.purpose: Separates the fixed-position Toast from whatever page content happens to sit beneath it — the other floating, untethered element allowed a resting shadow.

motion.name: transition-shadow
motion.purpose: Reserved for a future clickable card's hover-lift transition.

motion.name: transition-colors
motion.value: background-color 150ms ease, color 150ms ease, border-color 150ms ease
motion.purpose: Default state-change transition for buttons, nav links, table rows.

motion.name: toast-in
motion.purpose: Toast entrance: fade + 8px rise. Disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one primary action on a screen — Sign In, Save, Record Stock.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #1F6FEB; color: #FFFFFF; padding: 10px 20px; border-radius: 8px; border: none; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #1558C0; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #FFFFFF, 0 0 0 4px #1F6FEB; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Inline row actions — Edit, Rename, Reset — no border, no fill at rest.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: #1F6FEB; padding: 6px 12px; border-radius: 8px; border: none; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: rgba(31, 111, 235, 0.1); }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Dot + label pill for stock status: OK / Low / Critical / No Data. Color and text always paired, never color-only; unrecognized values fall back to the neutral No Data treatment, never to green.
components.css: .ds-status-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 2px 10px; border-radius: 9999px; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; } .ds-status-badge--green { background: #F0FDF4; color: #15803D; } .ds-status-badge--no-data { background: #F9FAFB; color: #6B7280; } .ds-status-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #16A34A; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Flat container with a hairline border. No resting or hover shadow — every card in the current app is a static display surface, not a click target.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #FFFFFF; border: 1px solid #E5E7EB; border-radius: 12px; padding: 16px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; } .ds-card__title { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; color: #111827; margin: 0; } .ds-card__meta { font-size: 0.75rem; color: #6B7280; margin: 0; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Text input with label, hairline border at rest, blue ring on focus, red on error.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; } .ds-field__label { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #111827; } .ds-field__input { width: 100%; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #E5E7EB; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #111827; background: #FFFFFF; transition: border-color 150ms ease, box-shadow 150ms ease; } .ds-field__input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: #1F6FEB; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(31, 111, 235, 0.4); }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Link
components.description: Active route gets a soft blue wash; inactive rows are muted with a paper-alt hover. Below the lg breakpoint this lives inside an off-canvas drawer triggered by a hamburger button, since the app is used on phones (Facility Worker role especially).
components.html: Dashboard
components.css: .ds-nav-link { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; padding: 10px 12px; border-radius: 8px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #6B7280; text-decoration: none; transition: background-color 150ms ease, color 150ms ease; } .ds-nav-link:hover { background: #F9FAFB; color: #111827; } .ds-nav-link--active { background: rgba(31, 111, 235, 0.1); color: #1F6FEB; }

narrative.overview:
Picture the board at the nurses' station: color-coded, glanceable, no wasted words, built to be read correctly in the two seconds someone has between other tasks. That's the system. Every screen exists to answer one question fast — is this facility's vaccine stock fine, low, or critical — and everything else is the supporting record for that one glance.

The system is clinical, trustworthy, and efficient by design, not by accident. It explicitly rejects the consumer/marketing-app register: no gradients, no playful illustration, no decorative motion. This is hospital-grade software used under time pressure — the interface should get out of the way of the actual task.
keyCharacteristics: Flat surfaces, 1px hairline borders, shadow only as a response to interaction or on the two floating elements (Modal, Toast)
One functional accent (clinical blue) reserved for actions and links; saturated color elsewhere is reserved for status meaning only
System font stack — no custom webfont
Status is always paired with a text label, never color alone, with a distinct neutral treatment for "no data yet"
Small, calm, forgiving surfaces — generous enough for a phone (the nav collapses to a drawer below the lg breakpoint), restrained enough for a dense table

rules.name: The Status-Only Saturation Rule
rules.body: Saturated color means one of two things: an action (clinical blue) or a stock status (red/amber/green). If a new element wants a bright color for any other reason, it's wrong — make it gray or make it blue.

rules.name: The System Font Rule
rules.body: No webfont is ever loaded. The interface should render instantly and disappear into the OS, not announce itself as designed.

rules.name: The Border-at-Rest Rule
rules.body: Every surface that's part of the page layout is flat with a hairline border when nothing is happening to it. Shadow only appears as a response to hover, or on the two floating elements (Modal, Toast) that sit outside the page's own layout.

dos: Do treat the red/amber/green/no-data quartet as sacred — always paired with a text label, and no-data never silently reads as OK or Low.
Do keep every laid-out surface flat with a hairline border; reserve shadow for hover feedback and the two floating elements (Modal, Toast).
Do size touch targets generously on Facility Worker screens — used on a phone, often one-handed, in a clinical setting.
Do write error messages that state the current true value ("Only 12 left"), not just that something failed.
donts: Don't introduce a second accent color.
Don't add gradients, glassmorphism, playful illustration, or any decorative motion.
Don't let a static (non-clickable) card carry a shadow.
